mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This dual constraint produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods (FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and inflation > 3.5%), S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers a negative equity risk premium adjustment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE increases > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced earnings growth expectations for equities. This demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth < 2% annualized, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at a rate above 2.5% annualized, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power for corporations. This supply strength supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Across all periods, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of instances when INDPRO growth is positive, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.5 million annualized units while simultaneously real GDP growth (GDPC1) slows below 1.5% annualized. This joint contraction in housing demand and overall economic growth signals late-cycle weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.5M annualized AND GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: In periods where both HOUST < 1.5M and GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable or rises, indicating a labor market inflection point. This employment weakness signals reduced consumer income growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100,000 AND UNRATE stable or rising
regime_hint: labor market deceleration with employment weakness
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 100,000 and UNRATE is stable/rising, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 2-month window, signaling an easing monetary cycle that reduces equity discount rates and improves financing conditions for corporations. This monetary easing produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprice risk assets upward.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp in 2 months, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 4% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) expand above 2.2 million annualized units, indicating robust consumer demand and housing market strength. This dual demand expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 4% annualized AND HOUST > 2.2M annualized
failure_condition: In periods where both RSAFS growth > 4% and HOUST > 2.2M, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
